title: Ypsilopus citrinus

intro: Ypsilopus citrinus, also known as the Yellow Ground Orchid, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia and Australia, characterized by its yellow flowers and long, narrow leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Ypsilopus citrinus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ypsilopus citrinus is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in the production of herbal teas and infusions.
